stemHtml: What action should a pilot take when suffering from a common cold, has no fever, but his/her nose is running and he/she is unable to clear his/her ears?

options.a: It is safe to fly even if the pilot is suffering from a common cold.
options.b: The pilot should not fly, because the infection could spread to the bronchi.
options.c: The pilot should not fly, because the fever could go up to such an extent that there is a risk of incapacitation during the flight.
options.d: The pilot should not fly, because there is a risk of not succeeding to equalise the pressure between the middle ear and ambient air.
